message.content:
POLARIS context: compose renders 346 baskets via OpenRouter (compose model = z-ai/glm-5.2, plus an
NLI verifier judge that shares the SAME OpenRouter account). Repo: /workspace/POLARIS (readable),
compose worktree: /home/polaris/wt/compose (writable).

THE DISPUTE YOU ARE SETTLING:
- Claim A (original Fable, baked into OVERNIGHT_MASTER_BRIEF.md:9): "compose is already ~48-way
  parallel; the real ceiling is the OpenRouter 429 rate limit."
- Claim B (later Fable gate, commit 0615bc5, backed by an A/B measurement): the ceiling was a
  missing await — _draft_passes_wrapper (generator/abstractive_writer.py:661) is a sync def that was
  called with NO await from the async coroutine _pre_pass_one_basket (:749), blocking the event loop
  on every NLI POST. True verify concurrency was 1. Measured 2.06s serialized -> 0.60s parallel.
  Under Claim B, the 429 could NOT have been binding pre-fix (you cannot rate-limit 1 in-flight req).

SECURITY: the OpenRouter API key is a secret. NEVER print it, never echo it, never paste it into
your result. Refer to it only as $OPENROUTER_API_KEY. Redact it if it appears in any output.

HONESTY: report REAL measured numbers. If you cannot measure something, say so. Do not estimate a
rate limit and present it as measured.


YOU ARE THE PROBE. Measure the REAL rate limit empirically. Recon found:
{
  "key_found": true,
  "key_location": "Variable name: OPENROUTER_API_KEY. Configured in the file /workspace/POLARIS/.env (single line `OPENROUTER_API_KEY=...`; the template is /workspace/POLARIS/.env.example:15). It is NOT in the shell env by default and there is no .env in /home/polaris/wt/compose. Every run loads it by sourcing that file: /home/polaris/wt/compose/launch_compose_gear_iter4.sh:12 does `set -a; . /workspace/POLARIS/.env 2>/dev/null; set +a`. Code reads it via os.environ at /workspace/POLARIS/src/polaris_graph/llm/openrouter_client.py:58 and roles/openrouter_role_transport.py:1228. Value never printed.",
  "account_facts": {
    "credits_remaining": "MEASURED via GET https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/credits: total_credits=2550, total_usage=2329.168087508 -> ~220.83 credits remaining. The /key endpoint reports limit=null and limit_remaining=null, i.e. this key has NO per-key credit cap; usage=2272.36 (key-scoped), usage_daily=88.28, usage_weekly=282.54, usage_monthly=368.20.",
    "is_free_tier": false,
    "rate_limit_interval": "10s -- but OpenRouter itself marks this field deprecated ('This field is deprecated and safe to ignore.'), so it is NOT a real published limit.",
    "rate_limit_requests": "-1 (i.e. no numeric request cap returned; the field is flagged deprecated). OpenRouter does NOT expose a concrete requests-per-interval number for this key. Any specific RPS/RPM ceiling anyone quotes for this account is NOT measured and is not obtainable from the API.",
    "raw_response": "{\"data\":{\"label\":\"sk-or-v1-[REDACTED]\",\"is_management_key\":false,\"is_provisioning_key\":false,\"limit\":null,\"limit_reset\":null,\"limit_remaining\":null,\"include_byok_in_limit\":false,\"usage\":2272.360166297,\"usage_daily\":88.283711174,\"usage_weekly\":282.540299187,\"usage_monthly\":368.201526559,\"byok_usage\":0,\"byok_usage_daily\":0,\"byok_usage_weekly\":0,\"byok_usage_monthly\":0,\"is_free_tier\":false,\"expires_at\":null,\"creator_user_id\":\"user_39Ya2npurVBXLBcvPUDnOfIlX5R\",\"rate_limit\":{\"requests\":-1,\"interval\":\"10s\",\"note\":\"This field is deprecated and safe to ignore.\"}}}  [key label redacted]"
  },
  "evidence_of_429": {
    "count": "17 real HTTP 429s TOTAL across EVERY log file on disk. Breakdown: 12 in the currently-LIVE run /workspace/compose_wt/outputs/s5_gear_iter4/compose.log (window 08:33:12 -> 14:51:34 = 6h18m, containing 3656 OpenRouter POSTs -> 429 rate = 0.33% of calls, ~1.9 per hour); 4 in /workspace/compose_wt/outputs/s5_sonnet_hands/compose.log (2h10m, 1183 POSTs); 1 in /workspace/compose_wt/outputs/s5_gear_iter3/compose.log (1h14m, 703 POSTs). ZERO 429s in s5_gear_iter1 (396 POSTs), s5_gear_iter1_pinned (794 POSTs), s5_gear_round_iter1 (23 POSTs), POLARIS/outputs/s5_gear/compose_iter2.log (145 POSTs). ATTRIBUTION: all 17 were logged by src.polaris_graph.llm.entailment_judge (the NLI verifier). ZERO 429s from the compose writer model (openrouter_client / z-ai glm-5.2). SEVERITY: every single one was logged as 'attempt 1/6' -- i.e. all recovered on the first retry; no 429 ever escalated past attempt 1 and none exhausted the retry budget. This is a sporadic 429, NOT a binding ceiling: it is nowhere near 'every other call'.",
    "logs_searched": "/workspace/POLARIS/logs (pg_cost_ledger.jsonl, session_log.md, bug_log.md), /workspace/POLARIS/outputs (incl. s5_gear/compose_iter2.log, fetch_corpus_replay_*.log), /workspace/compose_wt/outputs/* (s5_gear_iter1, s5_gear_iter1_pinned, s5_gear_round_iter1, s5_gear_iter2, s5_gear_iter3, s5_gear_iter4 [LIVE], s5_sonnet_hands), /home/polaris/wt/compose/logs. Search terms: 'HTTP/1.1 429', '429', 'rate limit'/'rate-limit'/'ratelimit', 'Retry-After'. Note: a naive grep for '429' over-counts badly (2/3 of hits are token counts like '429 reasoning tokens' / '1429 chunks'); only 'HTTP/1.1 429 Too Many Requests' lines are real.",
    "retry_after_values": "NO Retry-After header was ever observed. grep -ci 'retry-after' over every compose log = 0 hits. The only backoff value that appears is 'rate-limit backoff 15.0s', and it appears 19 times with NO other value ever seen. That 15.0s is CLIENT-SIDE, not the server: entailment_judge.py:216 _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_RATE_LIMIT_FLOOR_S = 15.0, and _rate_limit_backoff_s() (entailment_judge.py:486-509) returns exactly the floor when the exponential term <= floor and returns a JITTERED (non-round) value otherwise. A server Retry-After would be honored verbatim (line 503) -- the fact that every observed value is exactly 15.0 confirms OpenRouter returned no Retry-After. So: we have ZERO measured server-supplied rate-limit guidance. The '15s' figure is our own constant, not OpenRouter's."
  },
  "configured_concurrency": {
    "PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY": "24 -- SET explicitly at /home/polaris/wt/compose/launch_compose_gear_iter4.sh:29 (the launcher of the currently-running compose, pid 966951, `bash launch_compose_gear_iter4.sh` started 08:33). Code default would be 8 (abstractive_writer.py _DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY). Other launchers in the worktree set it to 3 (iter1/gear scripts), 24 (iter3/iter4), or 12 (iter5). NOT set in /workspace/POLARIS/.env.",
    "PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM": "30 -- SET explicitly at /home/polaris/wt/compose/launch_compose_gear_iter4.sh:30. Code default is 5 (/workspace/compose_wt/src/providers/llm_provider.py:79 `_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM = int(os.getenv(\"PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM\", \"5\"))`). This is the GLOBAL semaphore every LLM call acquires. NOT set in /workspace/POLARIS/.env.",
    "PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY": "NOT SET ANYWHERE -> falls back to the code default 4 (/home/polaris/wt/compose/src/polaris_graph/llm/judge_concurrency.py:56 DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY = 4). It is absent from /workspace/POLARIS/.env and absent from launch_compose_gear_iter4.sh (only prose in .codex/ design docs mentions a value of 2). THIS IS THE LOAD-BEARING FINDING: the NLI verifier's provider POSTs are capped by a PROCESS-GLOBAL BoundedSemaphore of 4 (acquire_judge_slot), so verify-side OpenRouter concurrency can never exceed 4 regardless of the writer's 24 or the global 30 -- and 4 concurrent NLI POSTs is not a plausible way to hit an account rate limit.",
    "_caveat": "The live process (pid 966951) runs as root, so /proc/966951/environ is unreadable to me (NO_SUDO). These values are read from the launcher script that is provably its parent (root pid 817298 `bash launch_compose_gear_iter4.sh`, and the pid 966951 cmdline `python -u scripts/run_s5_i3.py --out /workspace/compose_wt/outputs/s5_gear_iter4/...` matches that script's OUT path exactly). I could not read the env block directly.",
    "_note_on_48_way": "The '~48-way parallel' figure in OVERNIGHT_MASTER_BRIEF.md:9 reconciles as PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY=24 x PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=2 (launch_compose_gear_iter4.sh:37). That is WRITER-side fan-out only. The verifier/NLI side is separately clamped to 4."
  }
}

Write a small standalone probe script (put it in /tmp, NOT in a worktree) that sends real requests to
OpenRouter for the compose model and RAMPS concurrency (e.g. 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32) with tiny cheap
prompts (max_tokens ~8 — you are measuring the rate limiter, not paying for generation).

CAPTURE:
- the X-RateLimit-Limit / X-RateLimit-Remaining / X-RateLimit-Reset response headers VERBATIM
  (this is the single most direct answer to "what is the rate limit" — read them, do not infer)
- the concurrency / req-per-sec at which 429 FIRST appears
- the Retry-After value the 429 actually carries
- sustained throughput (req/s) and latency p50/p95

CRITICAL — CONTENTION: a root-owned run_s5_i3.py (PID 966951) may still be hitting the SAME account.
Check with `ps -p 966951`. If it is running, your numbers are CONTAMINATED — you are measuring the
limit MINUS whatever it is consuming. Report that loudly in contention_note rather than presenting a
dirty number as clean. Keep total spend trivial; this is a rate-limit probe, not a load test.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Standalone OpenRouter rate-limit probe. Ramps concurrency, captures headers verbatim.
NEVER prints the API key."""
import asyncio, json, os, statistics, sys, time
import httpx

KEY = os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "")
if not KEY:
    print("NO KEY IN ENV"); sys.exit(1)
MODEL = os.environ.get("PROBE_MODEL", "z-ai/glm-5.2")
URL = "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions"
HDRS = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {KEY}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
BODY = {
    "model": MODEL,
    "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Say OK."}],
    "max_tokens": 8,
    "temperature": 0,
}
INTERESTING = ("x-ratelimit", "retry-after", "ratelimit", "x-request-id", "x-openrouter")

results = []
hdr_samples = []

async def one(client, level, idx):
    t0 = time.perf_counter()
    try:
        r = await client.post(URL, headers=HDRS, json=BODY)
        dt = time.perf_counter() - t0
        hs = {k: v for k, v in r.headers.items()
              if any(k.lower().startswith(p) or p in k.lower() for p in INTERESTING)}
        if hs and len(hdr_samples) < 40:
            hdr_samples.append((level, r.status_code, hs))
        body_snip = ""
        if r.status_code != 200:
            body_snip = r.text[:300].replace(KEY, "[REDACTED]")
        results.append(dict(level=level, idx=idx, status=r.status_code, lat=dt,
                            retry_after=r.headers.get("retry-after"), body=body_snip,
                            t_end=time.time()))
    except Exception as e:
        dt = time.perf_counter() - t0
        results.append(dict(level=level, idx=idx, status=-1, lat=dt,
                            retry_after=None, body=f"EXC {type(e).__name__}: {str(e)[:150]}",
                            t_end=time.time()))

async def burst(level, n):
    limits = httpx.Limits(max_connections=level + 5, max_keepalive_connections=level + 5)
    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=90.0, limits=limits) as client:
        t0 = time.time()
        sem = asyncio.Semaphore(level)
        async def guarded(i):
            async with sem:
                await one(client, level, i)
        await asyncio.gather(*[guarded(i) for i in range(n)])
        wall = time.time() - t0
    sub = [r for r in results if r["level"] == level]
    ok = [r for r in sub if r["status"] == 200]
    c429 = [r for r in sub if r["status"] == 429]
    other = [r for r in sub if r["status"] not in (200, 429)]
    lats = sorted(r["lat"] for r in ok) or [0]
    p50 = statistics.median(lats)
    p95 = lats[min(len(lats) - 1, int(0.95 * len(lats)))]
    print(f"[conc={level:>2}] n={n:>3} wall={wall:6.2f}s  ok={len(ok):>3} 429={len(c429):>3} "
          f"other={len(other):>2}  thr={len(sub)/wall:5.2f} req/s  ok_thr={len(ok)/wall:5.2f}  "
          f"p50={p50:5.2f}s p95={p95:5.2f}s", flush=True)
    if c429:
        print(f"    !!! 429 at conc={level}: retry-after={[r['retry_after'] for r in c429]}", flush=True)
        for r in c429[:2]:
            print(f"    429 body: {r['body']}", flush=True)
    if other:
        for r in other[:3]:
            print(f"    NON-200/429 status={r['status']}: {r['body']}", flush=True)
    return dict(level=level, n=n, wall=wall, ok=len(ok), c429=len(c429), other=len(other),
                thr=len(sub) / wall, ok_thr=len(ok) / wall, p50=p50, p95=p95)

async def main():
    print(f"MODEL={MODEL}  ts={time.strftime('%H:%M:%S')}")
    summary = []
    for level in [1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32]:
        n = max(4, level * 2)
        summary.append(await burst(level, n))
        await asyncio.sleep(1.0)
    print("\n=== VERBATIM RATE-LIMIT-ISH RESPONSE HEADERS (samples) ===")
    if not hdr_samples:
        print("NONE. OpenRouter returned NO x-ratelimit-* / retry-after headers on any response.")
    else:
        seen = set()
        for level, st, hs in hdr_samples:
            k = json.dumps(hs, sort_keys=True)
            if k in seen: continue
            seen.add(k)
            print(f"  conc={level} status={st}: {json.dumps(hs)}")
    print("\n=== ALL RESPONSE HEADER KEYS SEEN (one sample 200) ===")
    print(json.dumps(ALL_KEYS, indent=1))
    print("\n=== SUMMARY ===")
    print(json.dumps(summary, indent=1))
    tot = len(results)
    n429 = sum(1 for r in results if r["status"] == 429)
    print(f"\nTOTAL REQUESTS={tot}  TOTAL_429={n429}")
    first = next((r["level"] for r in sorted(results, key=lambda x: x["level"]) if r["status"] == 429), None)
    print(f"FIRST_429_AT_CONCURRENCY={first}")

ALL_KEYS = []

async def probe_headers():
    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=60.0) as client:
        r = await client.post(URL, headers=HDRS, json=BODY)
        ALL_KEYS.extend(sorted(r.headers.keys()))
        print(f"[header recon] status={r.status_code} keys={sorted(r.headers.keys())}", flush=True)

asyncio.run(probe_headers())
asyncio.run(main())

 {
  "level": 1,
  "n": 4,

content.text: Probe complete. Key facts: the NLI judge uses the **same model** (`z-ai/glm-5.2`, entailment_judge.py:106), so my probe hit the exact code path the historical 429s came from. Total spend ~0.51 credits for 1184 requests.

input.method: Standalone probe at /tmp/or_probe.py (NOT in a worktree). Real POSTs to https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions, model z-ai/glm-5.2 (the compose model — and, verified, ALSO the NLI judge's model: entailment_judge.py:106 _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL = "z-ai/glm-5.2", so the probe exercises the exact path every historical 429 came from). Prompt "Say OK.", max_tokens=8, temperature=0. Key loaded by sourcing /workspace/POLARIS/.env into env; never printed, never logged (probe explicitly .replace(KEY,"[REDACTED]") on any error body). Closed-loop bursts at a fixed asyncio.Semaphore concurrency, n = 2x concurrency per level. Ramp 1/2/4/8/16/32, then escalated 48/64/96/128 when nothing broke. Every response header key was dumped verbatim rather than inferred. 1184 real requests total (128 + 672 + 384 across three runs). Total spend measured from GET /api/v1/credits before/after: total_usage 2329.168 -> 2329.679 = ~0.51 credits.

measured.headers_seen: THERE ARE NO RATE-LIMIT HEADERS. This is a measured negative, not a failure to look. The COMPLETE verbatim set of response header keys OpenRouter returns on a 200 from this account is: ['access-control-allow-origin', 'access-control-expose-headers', 'cf-ray', 'connection', 'content-encoding', 'content-type', 'date', 'permissions-policy', 'referrer-policy', 'server', 'transfer-encoding', 'x-content-type-options', 'x-generation-id']. NO X-RateLimit-Limit. NO X-RateLimit-Remaining. NO X-RateLimit-Reset. NO Retry-After. NO ratelimit-* of any spelling. The probe filtered on ('x-ratelimit','retry-after','ratelimit','x-request-id','x-openrouter') across all 1184 responses and matched ZERO headers. So the single most direct question -- 'what does the server say the rate limit is' -- has a definitive answer: the server says NOTHING. There is no published, header-exposed rate limit to read, which is consistent with recon's finding that /api/v1/key returns requests=-1 on a field OpenRouter itself flags deprecated.
measured.first_429_at: NEVER. Zero 429s in 1184 real requests, at concurrency 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 48, 64, 96 and 128, at a sustained measured 17.62 req/s. FIRST_429_AT_CONCURRENCY=None. I could not induce a 429 at 128-way concurrency, which is 5.3x the writer's configured PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY=24, 4.3x the global PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=30, 2.7x the '~48-way' figure in the brief, and 32x the verifier's actual PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY semaphore of 4. I did not push past 128 because the goal was to find the ceiling, and a ceiling that is not reached at 32x the real verify concurrency is already conclusively non-binding.
measured.max_sustained_concurrency: 128 concurrent in-flight requests, 256 requests, 100% success (256 ok / 0 429 / 0 other). Never found a concurrency ceiling. The only failure seen anywhere was a single client-side httpx ConnectTimeout during the 48/64/96/128 sweep -- a LOCAL socket/connection-pool artifact, not a server rejection, and notably not a 429.
measured.throughput_req_per_s: Measured (requests / wall-clock per burst): conc=1 -> 0.66 req/s | conc=2 -> 1.25 | conc=4 -> 1.55 | conc=8 -> 1.42 | conc=16 -> 2.72 | conc=32 -> 13.21 | conc=64 -> 11.06 | conc=128 -> 17.62 req/s. Peak sustained = 17.62 req/s with a 100% success rate. NOTE the shape: throughput does NOT scale linearly with concurrency (32-way already gives 13.2 req/s; quadrupling to 128-way buys only 17.6). That flattening is upstream provider queueing -- it shows up as LATENCY, not as 429. There IS a soft ceiling out there, but it is a saturation/queueing ceiling, not a rate-limiter ceiling, and it sits far above anything compose is configured to generate.
measured.latency_p50_p95: Per-level, successful requests only: conc=1 p50=1.50s p95=2.07s | conc=2 p50=1.36s p95=1.66s | conc=4 p50=1.26s p95=3.25s | conc=8 p50=1.60s p95=9.34s | conc=16 p50=1.61s p95=10.89s | conc=32 p50=1.51s p95=3.22s | conc=64 p50=1.84s p95=3.09s | conc=128 p50=1.60s p95=6.49s. p50 is remarkably FLAT (~1.3-1.8s) from 1-way all the way to 128-way -- the server is not slowing individual requests down as concurrency climbs. All the pain is in the tail (p95 blows out to 6-11s), i.e. queueing, not throttling.

input.contention_note: YES -- CONTENDED, and I am flagging it loudly rather than dressing the numbers up as clean. `ps -p 966951` confirmed the root-owned `python -u scripts/run_s5_i3.py ... --out /workspace/compose_wt/outputs/s5_gear_iter4/...` was RUNNING before the probe (ELAPSED 01:44:27) and STILL RUNNING after it, i.e. it was hammering the SAME OpenRouter account and the SAME model (z-ai/glm-5.2) for the entire probe window. So every throughput/latency number above is the account's capacity MINUS whatever that run was consuming -- they are a LOWER BOUND on true headroom, not a clean measurement. Two things follow, and note they point in OPPOSITE directions, so be careful which way you lean:  (1) The THROUGHPUT and LATENCY numbers are DIRTY and pessimistic. 17.62 req/s and those p95 tails are what was left over while a 24x2-way compose run was already in flight. Do not quote 17.62 req/s as 'the account's max throughput' -- the uncontended number is higher by an unknown margin. I cannot clean this up without killing a live root-owned production run, which I will not do.  (2) The ZERO-429 result is *strengthened*, not weakened, by the contention -- and this is the load-bearing point. I stacked 128 concurrent requests ON TOP OF an already-running production compose and STILL could not provoke a single 429. A rate limit that cannot be tripped by (live run + 128 extra concurrent) is not a limit that was binding at (live run alone). The contention makes the negative result more conservative, not less.  BOTTOM LINE ON THE DISPUTE: this probe FALSIFIES Claim A. The brief's assertion (OVERNIGHT_MASTER_BRIEF.md:9) that 'the real ceiling is the OpenRouter 429 rate limit' is not supported by any measurement I can produce: no rate-limit headers exist to read, and no 429 can be induced at 32x the verifier's real concurrency even under contention. It is consistent with Claim B. Two independent facts corroborate B: (a) recon found all 17 historical 429s were logged by entailment_judge and ZERO by the writer, and every one recovered on 'attempt 1/6' -- sporadic upstream noise, not a ceiling; (b) the verify side is clamped by a process-global BoundedSemaphore of 4 (PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY unset -> DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY=4), and pre-fix the missing await made TRUE in-flight verify concurrency 1. You cannot rate-limit a single in-flight request. The 429 was never the ceiling; the blocking sync call was.
